heading: Procedure
body:
To create an AWS IAM role using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas cloudProviders accessRoles aws create [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas cloudProviders accessRoles aws create](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-cloudProviders-accessRoles-aws-create).

Save the `AtlasAWSAccountArn` and `AtlasAssumedRoleExternalId`
field values returned by the command for use in the next step.

- Log in to your AWS (Amazon Web Services) Management Console.

- Navigate to the Identity and Access Management (IAM) service.

- Select Roles from the left-side navigation.

- Click on the existing IAM role you wish to use for Atlas access
  from the list of roles.

- Select the Trust Relationships tab.

- Click the Edit trust relationship button.

- Edit the Policy Document. Add a new `Statement` object
  with the following content.

  > **Note:** Replace the highlighted lines with values returned in the previous step.

  ```json
  {
     "Version":"2012-10-17",
     "Statement":[
        {
           "Effect":"Allow",
           "Principal":{
              "AWS":"<atlasAWSAccountArn>"
           },
           "Action":"sts:AssumeRole",
           "Condition":{
              "StringEquals":{
                 "sts:ExternalId":"<atlasAssumedRoleExternalId>"
              }
           }
        }
     ]
  }

  ```

- Click the Update Trust Policy button.

To authorize an AWS IAM role using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas cloudProviders accessRoles aws authorize <roleId> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas cloudProviders accessRoles aws authorize](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-cloudProviders-accessRoles-aws-authorize).

If the command succeeds, you can use the `RoleID` value when
configuring Atlas services that use AWS (Amazon Web Services).
